title: FedEx Places First Order for 15 Workhorse W56 Step Vans to Grow Zero-Tailpipe Emission Fleet
text:
Workhorse-FedEx Partnership: W56 Step Van Order

Article Date: September 9, 2024

Order Details: Quantity: 15 W56 step vans. Customer: FedEx (NYSE: FDX). Delivery Timeline: 2024. Vehicle Type: Battery-electric step vans for last-mile delivery.

Key Performance Metrics: Fuel efficiency: 31 MPGe (vs. 7 MPG industry average). Annual emissions avoided: approximately 607 metric tons per vehicle. Average annual mileage per vehicle: 31,875 miles.

FedEx Executive Perspective: The electric Workhorse W56 will be part of our story as we aim to transition our global parcel pickup and delivery fleet to all zero-tailpipe emissions vehicles by 2040.

Workhorse CEO Comment: This milestone demonstrates our highly efficient and high-quality EV chassis and body along with our excellent engineering, field support, upfit and operational capabilities.

Vehicle Features: The W56 incorporates an efficient eAxle electric drivetrain with regenerative braking, designed specifically for stop-and-go delivery operations and supporting FedEx's carbon neutrality goals by 2040.
